skill.name: bats-testing-patterns
skill.description: Guides agents in writing reliable Bats tests for shell scripts, including fixtures, assertions, mocks, CI setup, and shell compatibility checks.
skill.summary: Create maintainable Bats test suites for shell scripts and CI workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, URL, path traversal, and weak crypto patterns, but review found they occur in Markdown examples for Bats test authoring. No executable helper scripts, prompt injection text, data exfiltration behavior, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Include Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The skill contains many Bash and Bats snippets with command execution, command substitution, temporary directory creation, cleanup, and shell invocation. These are instructional examples for test files, not executable skill code, but users should review commands before copying them into a project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is clearly fenced Markdown sample code for Bats tests and installation steps. It is still relevant to disclose because copied snippets can execute local commands.

low_findings.title: Benign Relative Paths and Filesystem Test Fixtures
low_findings.description: The path traversal and filesystem findings refer to example fixture paths, script-under-test references, /dev/null checks, and temporary test directories. These are normal Bats testing patterns and do not indicate unauthorized filesystem access by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are inside examples that point from tests to local project scripts or test utilities. No evidence shows hidden file reads, writes outside examples, or persistence behavior.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Installation URLs
low_findings.description: The URL findings are references to Bats installation and documentation resources. No evidence found that the skill performs network requests or sends local data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and a git clone installation example. There is no executable network client embedded in the skill outside Markdown examples.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear on descriptive Bats and testing text, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, insecure random generation, encryption, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context shows general documentation and test names, not cryptographic APIs. The scanner likely matched unrelated words in prose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Examples in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples include shell commands such as package installation, command substitution, stubbing commands, invoking bash/sh/dash, and cleanup with rm -rf. These are legitimate for a shell testing guide but should be treated as examples only.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is real, but it appears in fenced tutorial snippets rather than runtime skill logic. The residual risk is user copy-paste misuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Fixture Examples in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples create, compare, source, and remove files under temporary directories and local fixture paths. This is expected in Bats tests and does not show malicious filesystem intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are scoped to test fixtures and temporary directories in examples. No evidence found of hidden destructive behavior in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Shell scripts often lack repeatable tests for failures, files, and external commands. This skill provides practical Bats patterns for assertions, fixtures, mocks, and CI execution.
actual_capabilities: Explains Bats test file structure, setup, teardown, and TAP-compatible execution.
Provides assertion patterns for exit codes, output text, files, permissions, and line-based output.
Shows fixture management with temporary directories, copied inputs, and generated test data.
Demonstrates mocks, command stubs, and environment variable overrides for shell tests.
Covers CI integration with GitHub Actions, Makefile targets, and parallel Bats execution.
Includes shell compatibility examples for bash, sh, and dash.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run tests or install Bats automatically.
Examples use placeholder functions that must be adapted to a real project.
It does not verify shell portability for project-specific scripts.
It does not provide full coverage tooling for shell scripts.

use_cases.title: Add Tests to a Shell Utility
use_cases.description: Create Bats tests for command output, exit codes, file changes, and failure behavior in a project script.

use_cases.title: Standardize CI Script Testing
use_cases.description: Add Bats execution to GitHub Actions or Makefile workflows so shell regressions are caught before deployment.

use_cases.title: Teach Test-Driven Shell Development
use_cases.description: Give a team reusable patterns for fixtures, setup, teardown, and dependency stubbing in shell test suites.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Bats Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one script and simple assertions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bats-testing-patterns skill to write basic Bats tests for my shell script. Cover success, failure, exit codes, and expected output.

prompt_templates.title: Add Fixtures and Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for file-based test data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bats-testing-patterns skill to design a Bats fixture layout for this script. Include setup, teardown, temporary directories, and expected output checks.

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Commands
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for isolated shell tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bats-testing-patterns skill to test a shell function that calls external tools. Show command stubs, PATH isolation, and environment overrides.

prompt_templates.title: Design a CI-Ready Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bats-testing-patterns skill to create a CI-ready Bats testing plan. Include test organization, Makefile targets, shell compatibility, and parallel execution.

output_examples.input: A script needs tests for successful output and missing arguments.
output_examples.output: A Bats test plan with separate cases for valid input, missing input, expected exit codes, and clear output assertions.

output_examples.input: A shell utility reads files from an input directory and writes results elsewhere.
output_examples.output: A fixture strategy using temporary directories, generated input files, cleanup steps, and comparisons against expected output.

output_examples.input: A deployment script calls curl, jq, and other system tools.
output_examples.output: A testing approach that stubs external commands, isolates PATH, verifies command behavior, and avoids real network calls.

best_practices: Keep each Bats test focused on one behavior and name it clearly.
Use setup and teardown to isolate files, environment variables, and temporary directories.
Stub external commands when testing script logic that should not depend on real services.
anti_patterns: Do not let tests depend on files left by previous test runs.
Do not call real production services from unit tests.
Do not combine unrelated success and failure checks in one test case.

faq.question: What is Bats used for?
faq.answer: Bats is used to write automated tests for shell scripts and command-line behavior.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete test files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide complete Bats test files, but project-specific paths and functions must be supplied.

faq.question: Does it run Bats tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples. The user or agent must run tests in the project environment.

faq.question: Can it help with CI setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes GitHub Actions and Makefile patterns for running Bats in automated workflows.

faq.question: Does it support mocking external tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows function mocks, command stubs, PATH isolation, and environment overrides.

faq.question: Is it limited to Bash?
faq.answer: No. It includes examples for bash, sh, and dash compatibility testing.
